description: Machine-readable guide for harnesses choosing ts-quality CLI commands. Keep this aligned with COMMAND_CONTRACTS and dispatch behavior in packages/ts-quality/src/cli.ts.

globalRules.latestPointerFallback: .ts-quality/latest.json is used only by selected read/projection commands when --run-id is omitted

commands.name: init
commands.purpose: Create starter ts-quality config/support files in a target repo.
writes: ts-quality starter control-plane files under the target repo
automationNotes: Use only when intentionally initializing or refreshing the target repo control plane.

commands.name: materialize
commands.purpose: Export author-authored config/support data into canonical runtime JSON artifacts.
automationNotes: Use before check when a boring JSON runtime control plane is preferred.

commands.name: check
commands.purpose: Run the evidence, mutation, invariant, governance, and verdict pipeline for an explicit changed scope.
automationNotes: Provide --changed, config changeSet.files, or config changeSet.diffFile; check fails closed without changed scope.
Run coverage and the target repo's normal quality gate before check when meaningful evidence is required.

commands.name: explain
commands.purpose: Render the explanation trail for a selected persisted run.
automationNotes: Prefer --run-id to avoid ambient latest selection.

commands.name: report
commands.purpose: Render markdown or JSON report projection for a selected persisted run.
artifacts: JSON report projection on stdout when --json is passed
automationNotes: Prefer --run-id; report --json includes additive decisionContext metadata.

commands.name: trend
commands.purpose: Compare the latest run with the nearest earlier comparable run.
automationNotes: Comparable trends require matching changed scope and evidence baselines.

commands.name: plan
commands.purpose: Render governance implementation plan for a selected persisted run or current config context.

commands.name: govern
commands.purpose: Render governance findings and related evidence for a selected persisted run or current config context.

commands.name: authorize
commands.purpose: Evaluate whether an agent has standing to perform an action against a selected run.
automationNotes: Prefer --run-id; default action is merge when --action is omitted.

commands.name: witness test
commands.purpose: Run one explicit proof command and write an execution witness plus receipt sidecar.
reads: test files named by --test-files when present
automationNotes: Use for narrow proof commands; do not replace focused witnesses with repo-global test runs.

commands.name: witness refresh
commands.purpose: Run all configured execution witness commands impacted by the current changed scope.
automationNotes: Pass --changed in harnessed automation unless changeSet.files or changeSet.diffFile supplies scope.

commands.name: attest keygen
commands.purpose: Generate an Ed25519 keypair for local attestation workflows.

commands.name: attest sign
commands.purpose: Sign a repo-local subject artifact with an Ed25519 private key.
automationNotes: Subject must be repo-local under --root; run-scoped payload metadata must match the subject path when present.

commands.name: attest verify
commands.purpose: Verify a signed attestation against trusted public keys.
artifacts: JSON verification record when --json is passed

commands.name: amend
commands.purpose: Evaluate and optionally apply a constitutional amendment proposal.
writes: constitution file only when --apply is passed and amendment is applicable
